sources_audited.note: The 2012 Census definition for 325194 lists wood or gum distillation, coal-tar distillation, wood or gum chemical manufacturing, and cyclic crude or intermediate manufacturing from refined petroleum or natural gas, matching the packet's claim.
The BLS NAICS 325 page reports the cited 2025 employment counts for chemical equipment operators, chemical technicians, chemists, mixing and blending operators, and packaging and filling operators.

The ILO brief reports 24 percent of clerical-support tasks at high automation exposure and 58 percent at medium exposure; it also reports 1 to 4 percent high exposure and no more than 25 percent medium exposure for other occupational groups.

The Census infographic reports 51 percent age 55 or older and identifies the 2019 Annual Business Survey, data year 2018, while explicitly limiting the estimate to responding owners of employer businesses.
The EPA page includes Cyclic Crude and Intermediate Production among nine regulated chemical-manufacturing area-source categories and states that amendments were finalized on March 28, 2026.
The EIA annual table reports 115.776 million barrels for 2019 and 74.983 million barrels for 2024 in the U.S. petrochemical-feedstock product-supplied series.

The AEO 2026 narrative says industrial liquid-fuel use grows across all cases, identifies HGL and naphtha feedstocks in bulk chemicals as drivers, and projects industrial petroleum use rising from 5.5 million barrels per day in 2025 to 6.3-7.7 million in 2050 in most cases.

caveats: The coherent but narrow toll/custom-processing lens is disclosed; the eligible share e remains an unvalidated estimate, and classifying a single firm differently would matter in a 12-firm dataset population.
The a proxy combines a broad NAICS 325 occupation mix with global occupation-level GenAI task exposure rather than a wage-weighted task inventory for eligible 325194 processors.

The rho, q, and o values are bounded judgments without direct eligible-firm deployment, contract, or make-versus-buy evidence; S5 documents regulatory exposure but does not measure implementation or transferability.
The s5 proxy uses cross-industry 2018 owner-age data as succession pressure, not observed control transfers, and the packet appropriately applies a substantial downward bridge.

The d5 proxy combines a broad petrochemical-feedstock history with a much longer-horizon industrial petroleum outlook; neither directly measures five-year constant-quality outsourced demand, and gum and wood chemicals are largely absent.
The margin-bridged n estimate is held fixed in the scenario envelope, so the published interval does not capture uncertainty in the very small eligible-firm denominator.
summary: Mechanics pass and every primitive-cited source was reopened. The cited facts are supported, evidence states and proxy bridges are honest, the narrowed lens is disclosed, and the ranges expose substantial uncertainty. The record is useful as a model-conditioned screen, but direct evidence on eligible firms, AI implementation, transfer frequency, retention, and five-year outsourced demand is weak. None of these caveats, corrected or honestly downgraded, would change the robust STRUCTURAL_OUT base tier or the VALIDATE_ASSUMPTIONS action.
